title: pleroma ciliare
side_effects: Pleroma ciliare can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches. If ingested in large quantities, it can cause stomach pain, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oil can lead to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. In severe cases, improper use of pleroma ciliare can even trigger cardiovascular issues, such as rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations.
